summary.intent: Fix FSM consistency issues in ars-components input components (PinInput, NumberInput, SearchInput) identified by a multi-pass Codex review.
summary.outcome: All 6 P2 FSM consistency findings from pass 3 were fixed, 8 new unit tests added, all 1974 tests pass, clippy clean, and changes committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a multi-pass Codex review workflow where findings are batched by pass and priority (P2 = medium priority FSM issues).
Input components (PinInput, NumberInput, SearchInput) are FSM-driven with explicit state transitions in mod.rs files under crates/ars-components/src/input/.
SearchInput uses a debounce mechanism with Cancel/Restart events; spec requires prop changes to restart (not just cancel) the debounce timer.
The project uses xci-fast as a CI-equivalent fast check before committing.
Specs for components live in spec/components/input/*.md and are updated alongside code fixes.

code.finding: DeleteChar arm required FSM fix; SetValue must trigger state transitions when pin completeness changes; SetProps must transition to a valid state when focus_index is invalidated.

IncrementToMax/DecrementToMin must round values; Blur must clear scrubbing state. f64 representation issue: 1.005 is stored as 1.00499... so round_to_precision tests must use values with exact binary representations.
on_props_changed must emit RestartDebounce (not CancelDebounce) per spec; required adding a new RestartDebounce Event variant.

workflow: Multi-pass Codex review catches regressions introduced by earlier fixes (round-2 fix #4 was flagged in pass 3).
Batching all findings from a single pass into one edit session before running tests is efficient.
When a test fails due to floating-point representation limits (f64), use inputs with exact binary representations rather than decimal round numbers.

friction: Codex pass 3 pushed back on a round-2 fix (debounce cancel vs restart), requiring a revisit and additional Event variant.
f64 precision caused a newly written test to fail — 1.005 is not exactly representable, so the test had to be rewritten with binary-friendly values.
